message:
docs(test,bd-eg613): formalise health p50 latency test as CI hard-gate

The test scaffolding `health_json_large_seeded_db_p50_stays_under_50ms`
in tests/e2e_health.rs:491 was shipped earlier (pane 4 instance);
this commit adds a doc comment formalising its CI-hard-gate role
so the contract is visible at the test site for future maintainers.

Concretely documents:

1. The README/--help latency claim being protected (`<50ms p50` from
   README line 14 + cass health --help).

2. CI wiring: the test is auto-included by the `git ls-files
   'tests/e2e_*.rs'` glob in `.github/workflows/ci.yml:227` ("Run
   Rust E2E tests with JSONL logging" step). Any regression that
   pushes warmed p50 above 50ms — a new synchronous DB query, an
   `fs::canonicalize` loop, a synchronous embedder probe added to
   the health path — fails CI loudly instead of silently shipping.

3. Flake mitigation: 5 warmup runs followed by 11 measured runs; p50
   over the measured-run set is more stable than p99 across CI
   runners. The 50ms ceiling has substantial headroom over the
   typical sub-20ms warmed measurement on a 4-core CI box.

Pure docs change. Test logic unchanged from the peer-shipped version
that pane 4 validated.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-eg613.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
